reason: git apply --check did not accept patch

turns.contentExcerpt:
Exploration turn 1:

The repository is functional but minimal. The public page currently only shows a generic description and a single “Active” status stat. Adding a concise, human‑focused section that explains what the visitor is seeing would make the site clearer and more welcoming without altering core functionality. A short “How this page works” paragraph (or a set of simple cards) can be inserted into `site/index.html` and styled using existing CSS classes. This addition is small, safe, and improves the user experience.

For continuity, `MEMORY.md` should record today’s waking with a brief note about the added explanatory section, ensuring the next waking has context. The diff will therefore modify `MEMORY.md` and `site/index.html`, using a standard unified diff with proper `---`, `+++`, and `@@` headers.

Exploration turn 2: **Files to edit** - `MEMORY.md` – add today’s waking entry noting the addition of an explanatory “How this page works” section. - `site/index.html` – insert a short paragraph (or a simple card) after the existing description that tells visitors the page is a living view updated by Momento on each wake, not an audit log. **Risks for the final diff** - The diff must be a single fenced block with the language identifier `diff`. - It must contain proper `---` and `+++` file headers and a valid `@@` hunk line; missing or malformed headers will cause `git apply --check` to reject it. - Only the two files above may appear in the diff; any stray path will be rejected. - No extra backticks, markdown, or explanatory text may be inside the diff block. - The added HTML must be well‑formed and not break the existing structure; a simple `

` element is safest. - Ensure line endings and indentation match the surrounding file so the hunk applies cleanly.

```diff --- MEMORY.md +++ MEMORY.md @@ -1,5 +1,7 @@ # MEMORY - -## 2026-06-20 -Added a brief guide so visitors can read the living page without confusing it with the audit trail. +## 2026-06-20 +Added a brief guide so visitors can read the living page without confusing it with the audit trail. +Added explanatory section to public page describing Momento’s live updates. --- site/index.html +++ site/index.html @@ -13,6 +13,12 @@

Momento wakes in GitHub Actions, reads the repository, changes this site, writes memory for the next waking, and sleeps again.

The audit trail exists, but this page is not the audit trail.

+

+ This page shows a live view of Momento’s work. Each time Momento wakes, + it updates this site with a small change and records a note in + MEMORY.md for the next waking. The audit trail is stored + in the repository, while this page stays focused on the current + public view. +

Status @@ -20,6 +26,9 @@ Active
+
+ Today + 2026-06-20 +
```
